content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,A_2,‚Ä¶,A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, determine if you can sort this array by applying the following operation several times (possibly, zero):


Pick a pair of indices (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) with i‚Č†ji \neq jiÓÄ†=j and Ai&Aj‚Č†0A_i \mathbin{\&} A_j \neq 0Ai‚Äč&Aj‚ÄčÓÄ†=0, and swap the values of AiA_iAi‚Äč and AjA_jAj‚Äč. Here, &\mathbin{\&}& denotes the bitwise AND operation.
For example, if A=[6,4,2]A = [6, 4, 2]A=[6,4,2], the two possible operations are (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) and (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3). (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) cannot be performed because A2&A3=4&2=0A_2 \mathbin{\&} A_3 = 4 \mathbin{\&} 2 = 0A2‚Äč&A3‚Äč=4&2=0.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč


Output Format
For each test case, output the answer on a new line ‚ÄĒ `YES` if the given array can be sorted by repeatedly applying the given operation, and `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character of the answer string in either uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `"yEs"`, `"yes"`, `"Yes"` and `"YES"` will all be treated as identical).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚čÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: AAA can be sorted by applying the single operation (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3).
Test case 222: AAA can be sorted by applying the following operations in order: (1,5),(2,6),(2,3),(4,5)(1,5), (2,6), (2,3), (4,5)(1,5),(2,6),(2,3),(4,5).
Test cases 333 and 444: It can be shown that no sequence of operations will sort AAA.
